fix: block bash redirect writes for read-only agent roles

Add detection for >, >>, tee, sed -i, patch, and dd patterns in bash
commands for acceptor, designer, and reviewer roles. File writes to
.agents/ are still allowed. This closes the redirect bypass vulnerability
where echo >> or sed -i could modify source files.

Skip embedding generation for ephemeral wisp working memory entries (#260)

* Initial plan

* Skip embedding generation for wisp-scoped working memory entries

Wisp working memory entries are ephemeral (30-60 min TTL), retrieved by
exact key, and cleaned up on completion. Embedding generation is
unnecessary and often produces warnings when chunks exceed the embedding
model's token window. BM25 fallback is sufficient for any search over
these entries.

Adds IsEphemeralKey check for the wisp/ key prefix in SetAsync and
corresponding unit tests.

commit.message:
fix: make CONF_TOKEN required to prevent saving without auth

CONF_TOKEN was required=False, allowing config to be saved without
completing QR auth or providing a manual token. This would cause
immediate LoginFailed on provider init. The QR action populates the
token before returning, so required=True works correctly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix invoke_agent span parenting so framework child spans nest correctly (#46069)

Add set_current_span/detach_context to TracingHelper so the invoke_agent
span is attached as the active OTel context span. This ensures child spans
created by framework handlers are parented under invoke_agent instead of
appearing as siblings.

- TracingHelper.set_current_span() attaches the span to context
- TracingHelper.detach_context() restores the previous context
- InvocationHandler propagates span_token through both streaming and
  non-streaming cleanup paths
- Added test_span_parenting.py to verify parent-child relationship

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix/responses span parenting (#46068)

* fix: set OTel span as current context for proper parent-child span relationships

The responses handler creates the OTel span via start_request_span() which
uses start_span() internally - a detached span not set as the active context.
This causes child spans from user handler code (e.g. Agent Framework) to appear
as separate root spans instead of children.

Use set_current_span() after creating the span so downstream code sees it as
the active parent. Detach the context when streaming completes or in the
finally block for non-streaming paths.

* fix(core): add flush_spans() to drain BatchSpanProcessor before sandbox suspend (#46181)

BatchSpanProcessor exports spans on a background timer (default 5s).
In hosted sandbox environments the platform may suspend the process
immediately after an HTTP response is sent, before the timer fires.
This causes short-lived spans (e.g. LangGraph per-node invoke_agent
spans) to be lost.

Add flush_spans() to the core public API and call it from
trace_stream's finally block so the streaming path also flushes.

Verified: same agent produces 19-31 spans with flush vs 11 without.

* fix: flush pending spans before HTTP response to prevent loss in hosted sandboxes (#46154)

BatchSpanProcessor exports spans on a background timer (default 5s).
In hosted sandbox environments the platform may suspend the process
immediately after the HTTP response is sent, before the timer fires.
This causes short-lived spans — such as LangGraph per-node invoke_agent
spans created by third-party tracers — to be lost.

Add flush_spans() to the core public API and call it:
- In _endpoint_handler.py's finally block (covers all non-streaming exits)
- In trace_stream's finally block (covers the streaming path)

Locally verified: same agent code produces 30 spans with flush vs 11
without flush, confirming the BatchSpanProcessor timing issue.

commit.message:
fix: remove BinaryOp/Compare feedback seeding (caused 100% deopt) + JIT entry before stacker + interpreter IC optimizations

CRITICAL FIX: Feedback seeding in maybe_compile_maglev/turbofan was setting
BinaryOp, Compare, and BinaryOpInc slots to Monomorphic without actual type
data. The graph builder saw Monomorphic state, tried to specialize, but had
no type info -> generated wrong code -> every execution hit deopt.

This caused fib_40 to regress 97x (0.6us -> 58.3us, tried=1000 deopted=1000).
Fix: only seed KeyedLoadProperty/KeyedStoreProperty (original intent).

Additional improvements:
- Move try_execute_best_jit before stacker boundary (fixes entered_delta=0)
- Stack-local inline cache for globals in SMI loop (4-entry, no hashing)
- Deferred generation bumping in StaGlobal (batched at JumpLoop)
- MegamorphicIc::probe with unchecked bounds (hot path optimization)
- dispatch_entry_diagnostics for debugging JIT entry issues
- Test for deep_object arrow function Maglev compilation

commit.message:
feat: add os field for platform-specific tool filtering

Add an 'os' field to tool config that restricts installation to
specific operating systems. When set (e.g., os = ["linux"]), the
tool is skipped on other platforms during install, upgrade, and
shown as 'skipped (os)' in list output. If omitted, tools install
everywhere (existing behavior).

This enables users to manage a tool through another package manager
on one OS (e.g., Homebrew on macOS) while using gh-tool on others.

  - Accepts path or json (mutually exclusive)

    translation_synthesized_fields, translation_unsupported_fields, translation_notes

- Add workflow preflight validation to comfyui_workflow resource
  - validate_before_execute: optional+computed, defaults true
  - validation_summary_json: computed, stores JSON validation report
  - Fetches /object_info and validates prompt before queueing when enabled
  - /object_info unreachable returns hard error

commit.message:
fix: add minimum frame to stats window to prevent layout loop on macOS 26 Tahoe (#33) (#34)

NavigationSplitView(.balanced) requires a concrete lower-bound width
during its first-responder layout pass (NSWindow.layoutIfNeeded).
Without it, AppKit on macOS 26 Tahoe re-enters
NSPerformVisuallyAtomicChange recursively, deadlocking the main thread.

Add .frame(minWidth: 600, minHeight: 400) to CompanionWindowView inside
the 'stats' Window scene. 600pt = 180pt sidebar min + 420pt detail,
matching the existing navigationSplitViewColumnWidth(min: 180) constraint.

The other two Window scenes ('session-complete', 'coach-intervention')
already carry .windowResizability(.contentSize) and are unaffected.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
